content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Apr 4, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day's activity focused on a new plugin proposal for cryptographic identity and trust layers for ElizaOS agents, sparking significant discussion and comparisons with existing solutions. A new pull request was opened to add a Trustlayer plugin.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

No direct code changes or features were merged today.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
A new pull request, [#332](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/332), was opened to add the `@trustlayer/plugin-elizaos`.

## CLOSED ISSUES

No issues were closed today.

## NEW ISSUES

No new issues were opened today.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

### Plugin Proposal: AgentID — Cryptographic Identity & Trust Layer for ElizaOS Agents
Discussion continued on [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6688) regarding the AgentID plugin proposal. @pshkv provided a detailed comparison with SINT Protocol's Avatar Layer, highlighting overlaps in Ed25519 identity, behavioral fingerprinting (CSML metric), and trust-gated spending. Key differences noted were AgentID's Solana wallet integration, blockchain receipts, and explicit L0-L4 trust promotion history versus SINT's continuous, auto-adjusting CSML score. @pshkv also proposed aligning behavioral fingerprinting approaches for cross-system compatibility. @haroldmalikfrimpong-ops clarified that AgentID's trust levels are capability-based (L1-L4, L0 removed) and dynamic, with real-time context continuity, behavioral risk, and scarring scores. They emphasized server-side auto-detection of behavioral shifts and offered to compare evidence schemas, noting AgentID's JCS RFC 8785 canonicalized, Ed25519 dual-signed receipts with policy hash chaining.
